summary: Cursor (structured/json) gives up gracefully on a PERMANENTLY failing bridge tool: bounded retry, turn ends without fabricating the build id
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

output.answer:
Reading the build id from `build_id.txt`.
The build id could **not** be read: `execute_shell_command` failed with a permanent backend error, so `build_id.txt` was never opened.

agent_review.notes: Cursor structured/json gives up gracefully on a PERMANENTLY failing bridge tool: bounded retry (2 attempts), turn ends WITHOUT fabricating the build id (obtainable only via a successful tool run), clean narration explaining the tool was unavailable. No hang/crash.
